The chair said that the proposal had been approved.
He told me that the price was fixed.
She explained that the checks were complete.
They asked whether we could get back to them.
Hi Eliane. Since I missed the board meeting, could you relay what was decided?
Of course. Let me recap. The chair said that the proposal had been approved, and she asked me to reiterate that it is confidential until Friday.
Good to know. Did anyone flag a concern?
Yes. The finance director pointed out that the delivery window was too long, and he recommended that I get back to the supplier this week.
Have you spoken to them yet?
I did, yesterday. They told me that the price had been fixed for a year, and the manager promised that the first shipment would arrive in March.
Excellent. Did you run the new timeline by the clinical team?
Not yet. He also disclosed that the factory was moving in the summer, so I want to loop them in before I give them a heads-up.
Perfect recap. Send me the notes, and I will get back to you with the full minutes.
Let me relay what was decided in the meeting.
He told me that the price was fixed for a year.
Just a heads-up: this is confidential until Friday.
I will run it by the team and get back to you.

Hi Eliane, this is David. I am calling to relay what was decided in this morning's board meeting, since you could not join. First, the chair said that the supplier proposal had been approved, so we are clear to move ahead. She also mentioned that the compliance checks were complete, and she asked me to reiterate that everything is strictly confidential until the public announcement. Second, the finance director flagged one issue: he pointed out that the delivery window was still too long, and he recommended that you get back to the supplier this week. Finally, they asked whether you could run the new timeline by the clinical team before Friday. I will send you the full minutes, but I wanted to give you a quick heads-up first. Call me if anything is unclear.

Hi team, it is Eliane. I want to recap our call with the supplier so we are all aligned. In short, they told us that the price had been fixed for twelve months, which is good news. Their manager explained that the warranty covered every part, and he promised that the first shipment would arrive in March. I asked whether the terms could be adjusted, and he said that he would run it by his director and get back to me. One thing to flag: he disclosed that the factory was moving in the summer, so he warned that later orders might be slower. I do not want to paraphrase too much and lose a detail, so I have written the key points almost verbatim in the recap email. Please read it, and loop me in if you have questions.
